extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each entity: I counted only the Against Company, Against the Directors and Against the Promoter rows, excluding all By rows. Criminal proceedings against the company, directors and promoter are each nil, so the criminal count is 0; statutory/regulatory proceedings against them are 18 + 6 + 6 = 30, while tax proceedings counted for the company only are 21, excluding the promoter/director personal tax matters. The quantified amount against the company is ₹197.97 million; no subsidiary amount is disclosed, so the group aggregate is ₹197.97 million, converted to ₹19.797 crore. The contingent-liabilities table states a Total of ₹162.96 million as of March 31, 2023, which is ₹16.296 crore; this stated total is used rather than summing line items. The customer concentration figures are company-wide top-customer percentages of revenue from operations for the full fiscal year FY2023: top 5 equals 40.67% (sum of the five disclosed customer percentages) and top 10 is the stated total of 49.62%. The source monetary unit is million, and conversion to crore is by dividing by 10 (₹10 million = ₹1 crore).
